reforms.description: Austria adopted a comprehensive strategy for research, technology and innovation in March 2011, which includes measures to improve and clarify the financing of public universities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving financing for public universities is expected to enhance research capacity and innovation output, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was adopted and includes various measures for public universities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant overhaul of the approach to research and innovation funding in Austria, aiming to enhance the overall capacity and effectiveness of the public research sector.
reforms.source_quote: In March 2011 Austria adopted a comprehensive strategy for research, technology and innovation (Strategie der Bundesregierung für Forschung, Technologie und Innovation), which includes different measures to improve and clarify the financing of public universities.

reforms.description: The Austrian federal government agreed on a road map for 2011-13 that includes measures to promote innovation, investment, and internationalisation for small and medium enterprises through grants and low-interest credit.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to SMEs, the reform aims to enhance their capacity for innovation and competitiveness, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government agreed on the road map and its measures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The road map represents a significant effort to enhance the innovation capacity of SMEs, which is crucial for economic performance, but it is not a structural overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The Austrian federal government agreed on seven work packages (road map 2011-13) containing more than 90 measures.

reforms.description: The Austrian government plans to enhance cooperation between science and industry, focusing on innovation-friendly public procurement and excellence in research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving collaboration between research institutions and industry can facilitate the transfer of knowledge and technology, leading to enhanced innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to enhance cooperation between science and industry.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for fostering innovation but does not represent a complete overhaul of existing systems.
reforms.source_quote: The focus on innovation-friendly public procurement, co-operation between science and industry, and excellence in research will be enhanced.

reforms.description: The National Capacity Plan (Österreichischer Strukturplan Gesundheit, ÖSG) was introduced to direct capacity building in Austria's health system, requiring each Land to produce a regional plan in cooperation with its regional Sickness Fund, aimed at improving efficiency and resource allocation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the efficiency and allocation of health resources, the plan aims to enhance overall health outcomes and reduce unnecessary expenditures.
reforms.status_evidence: The first version of the plan was finalized in 2006 and has been in application since then.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a national capacity planning framework represents a significant structural change in how health services are organized and delivered, with potential long-term impacts on efficiency and health outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: A new 'National Capacity Plan' (Österreichischer Strukturplan Gesundheit, ÖSG) is intended as a central strategic instrument.

reforms.description: A federal fund pool can be created to help finance innovation and structural change in care supply, aiming to better balance inpatient and outpatient services and contain costs.
reforms.package_name: Federal Fund Pool for Health Innovation
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By consolidating resources and focusing on innovation in healthcare, the reform aims to improve efficiency and effectiveness in service delivery, which can enhance overall health outcomes and reduce costs.
reforms.status_evidence: A federal fund pool may also be put in charge of hospital investment across the territory, according to national plan objectives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a federal fund pool represents a significant shift in how health resources are managed, aiming for better integration and efficiency in healthcare delivery.
reforms.source_quote: A federal fund pool can be created and utilised to help finance innovation and structural change in care supply.

reforms.description: Austria is developing the ELGA system, a publicly financed infrastructure for electronic health records, aimed at improving patient care by making health-related information centrally available to providers and patients.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of healthcare delivery, the ELGA system is expected to improve health outcomes and reduce costs in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The first ELGA application will be run in 2011.
reforms.importance_rationale: The ELGA system represents a significant overhaul of health information management in Austria, with potential widespread impacts on healthcare delivery and patient outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: The government made progress in implementing individual electronic health records (the ELGA system).
